vBRIEFInfo.description: Story vBRIEF for #1880 (orchestrator sub-agent dispatch doctrine — worker-owns-lifecycle, background dispatch, deliberate model routing); guidance-only slice; gate enforcement tracked in #1877.

plan.title: Orchestrator sub-agent dispatch doctrine — worker-owns-lifecycle + background dispatch + routing decision

narratives.Description: Encode three orchestrator dispatch rules surfaced by the 2026-06-22 #1878 session (Gaps C and D): (1) implementation workers scoped to drive-to merge-ready own PR + review cycle as one unit of work; (2) long-running workers dispatch independently/in the background (Cursor Task background path); (3) deliberate per-worker_role model routing before any dispatch (doctrine only — deterministic verify:routing enforcement is #1877). Docs/skills/templates only; no gate code in this slice.
narratives.ImplementationPlan: 1) Add the three rules as !/⊗ directives in templates/agent-prompt-preamble.md §9 (canonical dispatch envelope). 2) Update packs/skills/skills-pack-0.1.json swarm + review-cycle skill bodies; run task packs:render. 3) Extend AGENTS.md Multi-agent orchestration discipline (#954). 4) CHANGELOG [Unreleased] entry. 5) task check + pre-PR loop; single commit; PR; own review cycle to merge-ready.
narratives.UserStory: As a swarm orchestrator, I want explicit doctrine for worker lifecycle ownership, background dispatch, and model routing so I do not hand back at PR-open, block the conversation on long workers, or silently inherit the parent model — and so dispatched workers inherit the same rules via the canonical preamble and pack-rendered skills.

narrative.Acceptance: Three rules appear as !/⊗ directives in/near §9 Sub-agent spawn rules; unit-of-work boundary (drive-to merge-ready vs stop-at pr-open) is explicit.

items.title: Update pack source + render swarm and review-cycle skills

narrative.Acceptance: skills-pack-0.1.json updated; task packs:render regenerates SKILL.md files with worker-owns-lifecycle (swarm Phase 5→6) and background-dispatch generalization (review-cycle Approach 1).
AGENTS.md Multi-agent orchestration discipline carries the three rules; CHANGELOG [Unreleased] entry references #1880 and notes #1877 gate split.

items.title: Gates, PR, review cycle to merge-ready

narrative.Acceptance: task check passes; pre-PR loop clean; PR opened on fix/1880-orchestration-dispatch-doctrine; Greptile merge-ready (confidence >3, zero P0/P1, SHA match); NOT merged.

references.title: Issue #1877: Deterministic verify:routing single-dispatch gate (enforcement half)
